title: SARM2: Multi-Task Stage Aware Reward Modeling for Self Improving Robotic Manipulation
snippet: Fine-tuning vision-language-action (VLA) policies for long-horizon manipulation still relies heavily on behavior cloning, which requires costly high-quality demonstrations and keeps policies near the demonstration distribution. title: Failing grades soar as professors see greater AI usage, dwindling math skills in UC Berkeley computer science classes
snippet: 35.3% of CS 10 and 10.6% of CS 61A students received F's in spring 2026—far above department guidelines. Professors cite students' overreliance on LLMs (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini) for homework as the primary driver of academic dishonesty and grade collapse, raising urgent questions about AI and learning outcomes at UC Berkeley.
match_reason: websearch (news_article) — Directly concerns AI's impact on student learning and academic integrity at a UC campus, central to the committee's charge.

title: Frontiers in Education: The moderating role of academic integrity in the relationship between AI use and student outcomes
snippet: Peer-reviewed study examining how academic integrity moderates AI tool use by university students; finds ~88% of students at surveyed universities use AI-based virtual assistants for assignments. Relevant to policy design on AI use limits and integrity frameworks in higher education.
match_reason: websearch (publication) — Peer-reviewed research on AI use, academic integrity, and student outcomes in higher education, informing the committee's integrity and assessment work.
